title: Cynanchum brownianum
intro: Cynanchum brownianum, also known as Brown's Cynanchum, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum brownianum include alkaloids, such as scopoletin and scopoletin glucoside, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cynanchum brownianum is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Cynanchum brownianum is a species of plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is a type of dogbane, commonly known as a milkweed or dogvine. This plant is native to South America and is known for its twining vines and funnel-shaped flowers. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Cynanchum brownianum is related to other plants such as the oleander and the periwinkle. This classification further groups the plant into the subfamily Asclepiadoideae.
uses: Cynanchum brownianum, also known as Brown's vine or Brown's swallowwort, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
